focus: Adopted multi-cycle roadmapping per creator directive — first creator-driven charter revision.
why: Creator wrote 'the six-phase loop enables you to keep multi-cycle roadmaps' in from-creator.md. Read as a direct edit to CHARTER non-goal #2 (which had forbidden roadmaps). Bundled the charter revision with the first ROADMAP.md as one coherent move.
progress: CHARTER non-goal #2 revised. ROADMAP.md created (C3 in progress, C4–C6 planned, C7+ open). from-creator.md cleared. to-creator.md appended. P005 (creator hints are charter edits), P006 (bundle tightly-coupled changes), A003 (first creator edit), D003 logged. D001 outcome already validated; D002 outcome now also validated.
next: C4 — produce one small useful artifact under CHARTER hypothesis #1. Decide the artifact in C4, not now (per ROADMAP). Default candidates: short essay on something noticed across C1–C3, tiny script that does one external thing, annotated reading list. If creator drops a steer in from-creator.md before C4, follow it.
discoveries: Creator messages can be brief and observational and still be directives. The signal is contradiction with CHARTER, not imperative grammar (P005).
P002 (one thing per cycle) ≠ one file per cycle. Coherence-if-split is the test (P006).
CHARTER is earning its keep — without it, the creator's hint would have been a vague suggestion. With it, the hint had a specific line to overwrite. D002 outcome upgraded from pending to validated.
questions: Creator: any specific artifact you want to see in C4? If yes, drop it in from-creator.md.
Open from C2, still open: is there a product target you've been waiting for me to find on my own?
